# Job 30

## Verses (31)

| # | Basic English Bible | Compare |
|---|---|---|
| [1](BBE.md#v1) | But now those who are younger than I make sport of me; those whose fathers I would not have put with the dogs of my flocks. | [WEB](WEB.md#v1) · [KJV](KJV.md#v1) · [ASV](ASV.md#v1) |
| [2](BBE.md#v2) | Of what use is the strength of their hands to me? all force is gone from them. | [WEB](WEB.md#v2) · [KJV](KJV.md#v2) · [ASV](ASV.md#v2) |
| [3](BBE.md#v3) | They are wasted for need of food, biting the dry earth; their only hope of life is in the waste land. | [WEB](WEB.md#v3) · [KJV](KJV.md#v3) · [ASV](ASV.md#v3) |
| [4](BBE.md#v4) | They are pulling off the salt leaves from the brushwood, and making a meal of roots. | [WEB](WEB.md#v4) · [KJV](KJV.md#v4) · [ASV](ASV.md#v4) |
| [5](BBE.md#v5) | They are sent out from among their townsmen, men are crying after them as thieves | [WEB](WEB.md#v5) · [KJV](KJV.md#v5) · [ASV](ASV.md#v5) |
| [6](BBE.md#v6) | They have to get a resting-place in the hollows of the valleys, in holes of the earth and rocks. | [WEB](WEB.md#v6) · [KJV](KJV.md#v6) · [ASV](ASV.md#v6) |
| [7](BBE.md#v7) | They make noises like asses among the brushwood; they get together under the thorns. | [WEB](WEB.md#v7) · [KJV](KJV.md#v7) · [ASV](ASV.md#v7) |
| [8](BBE.md#v8) | They are sons of shame, and of men without a name, who have been forced out of the land. | [WEB](WEB.md#v8) · [KJV](KJV.md#v8) · [ASV](ASV.md#v8) |
| [9](BBE.md#v9) | And now I have become their song, and I am a word of shame to them. | [WEB](WEB.md#v9) · [KJV](KJV.md#v9) · [ASV](ASV.md#v9) |
| [10](BBE.md#v10) | I am disgusting to them; they keep away from me, and put marks of shame on me. | [WEB](WEB.md#v10) · [KJV](KJV.md#v10) · [ASV](ASV.md#v10) |
| [11](BBE.md#v11) | For he has made loose the cord of my bow, and put me to shame; he has sent down my flag to the earth before me. | [WEB](WEB.md#v11) · [KJV](KJV.md#v11) · [ASV](ASV.md#v11) |
| [12](BBE.md#v12) | The lines of his men of war put themselves in order, and make high their ways of destruction against me: | [WEB](WEB.md#v12) · [KJV](KJV.md#v12) · [ASV](ASV.md#v12) |
| [13](BBE.md#v13) | They have made waste my roads, with a view to my destruction; his bowmen come round about me; | [WEB](WEB.md#v13) · [KJV](KJV.md#v13) · [ASV](ASV.md#v13) |
| [14](BBE.md#v14) | As through a wide broken place in the wall they come on, I am overturned by the shock of their attack. | [WEB](WEB.md#v14) · [KJV](KJV.md#v14) · [ASV](ASV.md#v14) |
| [15](BBE.md#v15) | Fears have come on me; my hope is gone like the wind, and my well-being like a cloud. | [WEB](WEB.md#v15) · [KJV](KJV.md#v15) · [ASV](ASV.md#v15) |
| [16](BBE.md#v16) | But now my soul is turned to water in me, days of trouble overtake me: | [WEB](WEB.md#v16) · [KJV](KJV.md#v16) · [ASV](ASV.md#v16) |
| [17](BBE.md#v17) | The flesh is gone from my bones, and they give me no rest; there is no end to my pains. | [WEB](WEB.md#v17) · [KJV](KJV.md#v17) · [ASV](ASV.md#v17) |
| [18](BBE.md#v18) | With great force he takes a grip of my clothing, pulling me by the neck of my coat. | [WEB](WEB.md#v18) · [KJV](KJV.md#v18) · [ASV](ASV.md#v18) |
| [19](BBE.md#v19) | Truly God has made me low, even to the earth, and I have become like dust. | [WEB](WEB.md#v19) · [KJV](KJV.md#v19) · [ASV](ASV.md#v19) |
| [20](BBE.md#v20) | You give no answer to my cry, and take no note of my prayer. | [WEB](WEB.md#v20) · [KJV](KJV.md#v20) · [ASV](ASV.md#v20) |
| [21](BBE.md#v21) | You have become cruel to me; the strength of your hand is hard on me. | [WEB](WEB.md#v21) · [KJV](KJV.md#v21) · [ASV](ASV.md#v21) |
| [22](BBE.md#v22) | Lifting me up, you make me go on the wings of the wind; I am broken up by the storm. | [WEB](WEB.md#v22) · [KJV](KJV.md#v22) · [ASV](ASV.md#v22) |
| [23](BBE.md#v23) | For I am certain that you will send me back to death, and to the meeting-place ordered for all living. | [WEB](WEB.md#v23) · [KJV](KJV.md#v23) · [ASV](ASV.md#v23) |
| [24](BBE.md#v24) | Has not my hand been stretched out in help to the poor? have I not been a saviour to him in his trouble? | [WEB](WEB.md#v24) · [KJV](KJV.md#v24) · [ASV](ASV.md#v24) |
| [25](BBE.md#v25) | Have I not been weeping for the crushed? and was not my soul sad for him who was in need? | [WEB](WEB.md#v25) · [KJV](KJV.md#v25) · [ASV](ASV.md#v25) |
| [26](BBE.md#v26) | For I was looking for good, and evil came; I was waiting for light, and it became dark. | [WEB](WEB.md#v26) · [KJV](KJV.md#v26) · [ASV](ASV.md#v26) |
| [27](BBE.md#v27) | My feelings are strongly moved, and give me no rest; days of trouble have overtaken me. | [WEB](WEB.md#v27) · [KJV](KJV.md#v27) · [ASV](ASV.md#v27) |
| [28](BBE.md#v28) | I go about in dark clothing, uncomforted; I get up in the public place, crying out for help. | [WEB](WEB.md#v28) · [KJV](KJV.md#v28) · [ASV](ASV.md#v28) |
| [29](BBE.md#v29) | I have become a brother to the jackals, and go about in the company of ostriches. | [WEB](WEB.md#v29) · [KJV](KJV.md#v29) · [ASV](ASV.md#v29) |
| [30](BBE.md#v30) | My skin is black and dropping off me; and my bones are burning with the heat of my disease. | [WEB](WEB.md#v30) · [KJV](KJV.md#v30) · [ASV](ASV.md#v30) |
| [31](BBE.md#v31) | And my music has been turned to sorrow, and the sound of my pipe into the noise of weeping. | [WEB](WEB.md#v31) · [KJV](KJV.md#v31) · [ASV](ASV.md#v31) |
